search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng217 first seizure assessment history examination ECG imaging information safety
candidate_sources_rejected: anti-seizure medicine, rescue dose and driving instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A seizure presentation does not automatically establish epilepsy or type.
No medicine, dose, treatment, driving or patient instruction is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children, young people and adults with a suspected or confirmed seizure; pregnancy, neonates, status, intoxication and acute symptomatic causes require additional pathways.
setting_applicability: Emergency initial seizure assessment and communication.
UAE_applicability: Assessment domains transfer, but UAE emergency, neurology, driving, medicine, imaging, laboratory and follow-up policies apply.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG217 registered recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE seizure assessment section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, risk context, measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance, time-critical features and existing tests without diagnosing epilepsy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports seizure-event and witness history, physical examination, ECG or metabolic context, EEG review, MRI review, recurrence after remission, and specialist assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact information section supports a clinician-entered plan, completed risk discussion and arranged specialist or follow-up communication only when discussed; it does not generate safety advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports trigger, adherence, side-effect, safety, driving, employment or education, reproductive-health, and care-plan discussions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Emergency and cluster seizure plans were reviewed only for clinician-confirmed existing plans; no medicine, dose or automatic emergency plan was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of seizure clusters or convulsive emergencies and a clinician-agreed individual emergency management plan without mapping emergency medication or doses.

unresolved_source_gaps: Witness account, duration, recovery, previous events, fever, pregnancy, substance/medicine context, glucose, ECG, neurological detail, diagnosis, treatment, dose, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
